actionable_feedback: Add an explicit, machine-readable export of all workbook formulas (a plain text file listing cell addresses and their exact formula text) as [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] in the artifacts/ directory.

Add a 'Formulas' worksheet inside the workbook that contains the exact formula text for the key computed cells (event_density, green/amber/red logical cells, traffic_light decision cell) and the mapping to the packet_values source row/cell (i.e., show which embedded_packet fields feed each formula).

Produce screenshots (PNG) of the key formula cells and the 'packet_values' mapping tab (or the Stage_Assessment table) and save them under [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] to assist human verifiers who cannot open the workbook programmatically.

Add an explicit units annotation in the workbook and in the recommendation memo for 'event_density_1000' showing the exact formula including units (for example: =event_count / (injected_m3 / 1000) and a note that injected_m3 is in m3 so density is events per 1000 m3).

Optionally include a small plain-text audit table (CSV or Markdown) that shows the stage inputs, computed numeric intermediate values (event_density, boolean flags for green/amber/red), and final traffic_light text for each stage so verifiers can validate the numeric path without parsing formulas.

Update the recommendation memo (stimulation_hold_recommendation.md) to reference the new formula export, screenshots, and audit table, and include explicit citations to the workbook cell addresses used for each stage's decision.
artifact_specific_comments: microseismic_traffic_light.xlsx: Grader/trace claim that the workbook was created with embedded formulas (OOXML writer), but the verifier environment could not parse the XLSX (openpyxl_unavailable). Provide a complementary plain-text export of formulas and screenshots so the claim can be independently verified.

stimulation_hold_recommendation.md: Well-structured and documents the rules and outcomes. Update it to reference the additional verification artifacts (formula export, screenshots, audit CSV) and to include the explicit event_density formula text and units annotation.
feedback_summary: All required deliverables are present and the recommendation memo documents the decision logic, stage-level outcomes, sensitivity checks, and controlled-variable diagnosis. However, the verifier could not parse the XLSX in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) and therefore could not directly confirm that the workbook contains the claimed embedded, executable formulas and exact cell-level formula text. Provide easily-verifiable formula evidence (exported formula text and/or screenshots and an internal 'Formulas' audit sheet) and a short clarification of units for the 'Event density /1000 m3' column so the verifier can confirm semantic claims without relying on the grader trace alone.

extra_model_fields.preferred_verification_instructions: Include full cell address -> formula text exports; include screenshots of formula bar showing formula text; list checksums (sha256) for the exported formula text and for the XLSX so verifiers can confirm artifact integrity.
extra_model_fields.verifier_issue_summary: unsupported_claim: Unable to parse XLSX to confirm embedded formulas; supported only by artifact presence and agent trace.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt (606-revised) which adds: (1) [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] containing exact cell formula text; (2) [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] PNGs of the key formula cells and packet_values mapping; (3) a 'Formulas' sheet inside the workbook with formula text and cell-to-packet mapping; (4) a plain-text audit CSV summarizing computed intermediate values per stage; and (5) an updated recommendation memo that cites the new verification artifacts and clarifies units for event density.
trace_specific_comments: Trace shows two failed attempts with openpyxl (SyntaxError, then ModuleNotFoundError) followed by a library-free OOXML writer step ('generate_xlsx_minimal_ooxml') that claims to have embedded formulas. Because the verifier could not open the XLSX, include explicit exports/screenshots as evidence of those embedded formulas.

Please ensure the trace for the revised attempt records creation of the formula-export text file and screenshots and indicates the exact files produced (artifact paths and checksums) so verifiers can find them easily.
